title: Attack Of The Clothes

jokes.text: Well, what'll it be, folks? Tap water or bottled? Whatever. Kif, you pick. Oh, oh, it's always so hard to decide. Tap. I mean bottled. Bam!
jokes.setup: Simple choice between tap water and bottled water
jokes.punchline: Kif agonizes over the decision, changes his mind immediately, then Zapp acts like he made a decisive choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - shows Kif's neurotic indecision and Zapp's overconfident delegation in a mundane situation

jokes.text: Ooh, I love your boots, Leela. Nobody would ever guess they're knockoffs. How could you tell? I guessed.
jokes.setup: Amy compliments Leela's boots, saying nobody would guess they're knockoffs
jokes.punchline: When asked how she could tell, Amy deadpans 'I guessed'
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural setup/payoff with perfect economy. The logic is both absurd and perfectly reasonable

jokes.text: Like how I might say I like Amy's dress 'cause it covers all her tramp stamps.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining how girlfriends talk trash to each other politely
jokes.punchline: His example is brutally specific about Amy's tattoos
jokes.explanation: Solid burn with character specificity, but fairly straightforward insult comedy

jokes.text: Now, enough about who is and who isn't a Chinese skank. We're just two happy couples on a double date.
jokes.setup: Trying to defuse tension between the women
jokes.punchline: Zapp's idea of diplomatic language includes casual ethnic slurs
jokes.explanation: Very much in character for Zapp's tone-deaf 'diplomacy' but relies on offensive language for shock value

jokes.text: Let's go! Let's go! We've got to get these moon rocks to the moon by moondown.
jokes.setup: Urgency about delivering moon rocks
jokes.punchline: 'moondown' as parallel to 'sundown' when they're going TO the moon
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - time urgency and lunar setting

jokes.text: Oh, those lunar crybabies claim the rocks were stolen by the Apollo astronauts. They consider them part of their cultural heritage.
jokes.setup: Explaining why moon rocks need to go to the moon
jokes.punchline: Moon people claiming Earth-taken moon rocks as cultural heritage
jokes.explanation: Smart satire of cultural appropriation debates with sci-fi twist that feels earned

jokes.text: Nice work, Leela. You're really pulling your weight. Which is saying something. Ow!
jokes.setup: Bender compliments Leela on her work
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it with a weight joke, gets hit
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - can't help himself from the insult even when being nice. Good structure and consequences

jokes.text: I don't even need my over-the-shoulder boulder holder.
jokes.setup: Talking about light gravity on the moon
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for bra using boulder/holder rhyme
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works in context, though fairly obvious once you hear it

jokes.text: With one-sixth gravity, you can work and be lazy at the same time. It's like being a voice actor.
jokes.setup: Explaining the benefits of low gravity work
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to voice acting (which this show uses)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that works on multiple levels - commenting on the medium while making a physics joke

jokes.text: It's a necessity of the sport, it is.
jokes.setup: Explaining why butterfly derby contestants wear skimpy outfits
jokes.punchline: Obviously false justification delivered with folksy conviction
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing transparent excuse-making with folksy dialect

jokes.text: This is what I was expecting.
jokes.setup: After seeing the violent 'Murderflies' team introduced
jokes.punchline: Leela's unsurprised deadpan reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to her earlier 'not what I was expecting' line with economical delivery

jokes.text: You see that there? That's called a floor. Once a butterfly hits that, she's out.
jokes.setup: Explaining complex sport rules
jokes.punchline: Over-explains the concept of 'floor' in condescending tone
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing hillbilly condescension by over-explaining obvious concepts

jokes.text: Keep your eye on the ground. That's where she's going to land. Yep, called it.
jokes.setup: Predicting where a falling butterfly will land
jokes.punchline: Obvious prediction (ground) delivered as expert analysis
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about sports commentary stating the obvious with false expertise

jokes.text: Judging from your tank top, you love getting hooted at by hillbillies. And you could use the $50 for another gallon of Hello Kitty perfume.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to convince Leela to compete
jokes.punchline: Specific insults about Leela's clothing choices and perfume habits
jokes.explanation: Character-specific insults with good detail, but fairly straightforward mean-girl comedy

jokes.text: The bicker sisters teaming up? Talk about cat women on the moon.
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela decide to team up despite arguing
jokes.punchline: Play on 'Cat Women on the Moon' B-movie title
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to classic B-movie that fits the situation perfectly

jokes.text: Then prove it. By fighting.
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela claim they're not really fighting
jokes.punchline: Fry's solution to prove they're not fighting is to fight
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical contradiction delivered with maximum economy. Classic Fry logic

jokes.text: Why don't you and I ever do things together?
jokes.setup: Watching Amy and Leela team up for dangerous sport
jokes.punchline: Kif's sad realization about his relationship with Zapp
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Kif's longing for connection with oblivious Zapp

jokes.text: Amy, remember when we tried out for the Rockettes? And we failed because we accidentally kicked those two Rockettes to death?
jokes.setup: Leela reminiscing about past shared experience
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of accidentally killing people during auditions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy escalation - starts nostalgic, ends with casual murder. Great Futurama absurdist violence

jokes.text: We'll teach you not to lose immediately.
jokes.setup: Threatening the newcomers
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic - teaching them NOT to lose immediately (implying they'll lose slowly)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay threat that's both menacing and absurd through logical contradiction

jokes.text: Yay. They can move their arms.
jokes.setup: After Amy and Leela's poor performance
jokes.punchline: Extremely low bar for celebration - basic motor function
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan commentary on how badly they performed by celebrating minimal competence

jokes.text: Nothing that doesn't happen to everyone eventually.
jokes.setup: Asked what happened to the last big thing
jokes.punchline: Ominous non-answer implying death while being technically true
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - technically truthful but clearly ominous. Great character voice for shady promoter

jokes.text: And so began the fight career of two skimpy outfits and the feisty women who filled them.
jokes.setup: Narrator describing their career beginning
jokes.punchline: Focus on the outfits as the primary subjects with women as accessories
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of subject/object in sports commentary, subtly commenting on objectification

jokes.text: And although sometimes they got beaten, other times, they got beaten badly.
jokes.setup: Narrator describing their varied performance
jokes.punchline: False variety - both options are just different degrees of losing
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure and economy. Sets up expectation of variety then deflates it brilliantly

jokes.text: But just when it looked like they'd never win... They didn't.
jokes.setup: Classic 'but just when...' sports narrative setup
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by confirming the negative
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection that perfectly subverts sports movie tropes. Excellent structure and timing

jokes.text: Even that team that turned out to be us in the mirror.
jokes.setup: Listing all the teams they lost to
jokes.punchline: They lost to their own reflection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation showing just how bad they are. Original and perfectly ridiculous

jokes.text: Whoa, that puts the lead in my pencil. Thanks, trainer.
jokes.setup: Amy trying the nectar supplement
jokes.punchline: Using masculine idiom about arousal/energy for performance enhancement
jokes.explanation: Decent character work with Amy using inappropriate masculine phrases, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: Or... Inject it between your toes. Either way works, right?
jokes.setup: Asked if they just drink the performance enhancer
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests injection in a spot associated with hard drug use
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the shady nature of the 'supplements' with drug reference

jokes.text: Mmm! A guy could get used to this.
jokes.setup: Amy drinking/taking the nectar
jokes.punchline: Amy referring to herself as 'a guy' while clearly being female
jokes.explanation: Minor character quirk showing Amy's casual use of masculine language, but not particularly strong

jokes.text: That's pretty much it.
jokes.setup: After giving a detailed sports report
jokes.punchline: Robot sportscaster's complete lack of elaboration or analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect robotic character comedy - subverts expectation of sports analysis with mechanical literalness

jokes.text: So are carrots, but you don't see me injecting them between my toes.
jokes.setup: Professor arguing against the 'all-natural' defense of nectar
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of injecting vegetables, callbacks toe injection method
jokes.explanation: Good callback with absurdist escalation. Professor's logical argument taken to ridiculous extreme

jokes.text: I already got a double date with arm and hammer.
jokes.setup: Fry asking Leela out
jokes.punchline: Leela saying she's busy with her muscles (arm and hammer = biceps/strength)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Leela's changing priorities due to the nectar. Good character integration

jokes.text: Oh, so now I'm your last-resort booty call, huh? Okay, let's go.
jokes.setup: Fry asking Bender to hang out after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Bender calls out being second choice but immediately agrees anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - complains but has no standards. Good character consistency with sexual terminology

jokes.text: Cork your face bladder. I'm working!
jokes.setup: Kif trying to be affectionate during Amy's match
jokes.punchline: Amy's hostile response using weird anatomical terminology for mouth
jokes.explanation: Great character work showing Amy's personality change plus creative insult. 'Face bladder' is memorably weird

jokes.text: I got a little boy at home, and he's dying. Dying to see me kill these two.
jokes.setup: Seeming to invoke sympathy with dying child
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just enthusiasm for violence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure that subverts expectation from tragic to threatening

jokes.text: But from neglect. 'Cause you're going home in an urn. A funerary urn.
jokes.setup: Responding to threat about the opponent's dying son
jokes.punchline: Counter-threat about child neglect, then over-explaining 'urn'
jokes.explanation: Decent trash talk but the over-explanation of 'funerary urn' feels forced and unnecessary

jokes.text: She just wants to hit me with various chairs.
jokes.setup: Kif complaining about Amy's behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about furniture-based violence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing relationship deterioration with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: Hey Leela, want to know what the most beautiful thing is? No. Let's get hopped up.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to be romantic about the scenic planet
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately shuts him down to focus on drugs
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how the addiction has changed Leela's priorities. Efficient setup/payoff

jokes.text: Suck them, burn them... As long as they're not butterflies, I don't give a grand crap.
jokes.setup: Park ranger explaining environmental protection
jokes.punchline: Complete indifference to everything except butterflies
jokes.explanation: Good satire of single-issue environmentalists with colorful language

jokes.text: He can be identified by his orange coloration and enormous butterjunk.
jokes.setup: Scientific description of dangerous male butterfly
jokes.punchline: 'Butterjunk' as crude term for butterfly genitalia
jokes.explanation: Crude but creative portmanteau that sounds scientific while being juvenile

jokes.text: What's he gonna do, migrate at us?
jokes.setup: Warning about dangerous male butterfly
jokes.punchline: Bender minimizes threat using butterfly-specific verb
jokes.explanation: Clever use of butterfly behavior as threat, showing Bender's dismissive attitude with good wordplay

jokes.text: But believe me, you'll rue the day you came to the Butterfly Preserve. Well, enjoy the Butterfly Preserve.
jokes.setup: Ominous warning about terrible consequences
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to cheerful customer service voice
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing bureaucratic customer service conflicting with genuine warnings

jokes.text: I don't even know why we're helping find an addictive drug for our mean girlfriends.
jokes.setup: Fry and Kif searching for nectar
jokes.punchline: Fry suddenly realizes how absurd their situation is
jokes.explanation: Good observational moment where character realizes the obvious problem they're enabling

jokes.text: It smells like a skunk stuffed with feta cheese.
jokes.setup: Butterfly sprays defensive odor
jokes.punchline: Specific, disgusting comparison
jokes.explanation: Vivid, gross imagery that's specific enough to be funny rather than generic

jokes.text: Thank God I don't have a nose.
jokes.setup: Everyone reacting to terrible smell from butterfly spray
jokes.punchline: Bender's relief at his robotic anatomy advantage
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific relief. Uses Bender's robotic nature as practical advantage

jokes.text: Wait, you're flies. Don't you eat excrement? That's a day at the beach compared to this.
jokes.setup: Kif's fly parents complaining about Fry's smell
jokes.punchline: Even creatures that eat feces find Fry's odor worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing just how bad the smell is using flies' dietary habits as comparison

jokes.text: You smell kind of nice.
jokes.setup: Everyone else finds Fry's smell revolting
jokes.punchline: Amy (affected by nectar) finds the horrible smell attractive
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the pheromone plot with ironic contrast to everyone else's reaction

jokes.text: But McGruff the Crime Dog says... Oh!
jokes.setup: Amy telling Fry to keep his door unlocked
jokes.punchline: Fry starts citing safety PSA then realizes the implication
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Fry's childish knowledge base conflicting with adult situation

jokes.text: I'm done polishing my ass, Fry. Here's your toothbrush back.
jokes.setup: Bender returning Fry's toothbrush
jokes.punchline: Reveals he used it for extremely inappropriate purpose
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender grossness - using friend's hygiene items inappropriately. Solid character consistency

jokes.text: As God is my witness, I'll never forgive you for this!
jokes.setup: Finding Amy in bed with Fry
jokes.punchline: Leela's dramatic Gone with the Wind-style declaration
jokes.explanation: Decent melodramatic reference showing Leela's tendency toward dramatic declarations

jokes.text: This planet's got it going on.
jokes.setup: Chaos erupting with fighting and romantic drama
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual approval of the mayhem
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - enjoying chaos and drama that bothers everyone else

jokes.text: Sweet orgy of Georgia! Have you girls gone wild?
jokes.setup: Professor walking in on the romantic chaos
jokes.punchline: Old-timey exclamation mixed with modern reference
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character work mixing his old-fashioned speech patterns with contemporary references

jokes.text: Sweet orgy of Georgia! Have you girls gone wild?
jokes.setup: Professor sees the romantic chaos
jokes.punchline: Bizarre exclamation mixing 'Sweet Georgia' with 'orgy'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Professor exclamation that's both inappropriate and oddly charming

jokes.text: Oh, I think I'm getting a little caterpilly.
jokes.setup: Amy going through withdrawal symptoms
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining 'caterpillar' feelings with withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Creative neologism that works well for the butterfly theme and withdrawal symptoms

jokes.text: All right, let's get this party ended.
jokes.setup: Team preparing to face the final match
jokes.punchline: Leela's pessimistic rally cry subverting 'let's get this party started'
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of motivational phrase showing their realistic expectations

jokes.text: I don't give a crap!
jokes.setup: Celebrating kicking nectar addiction
jokes.punchline: Amy's hostile response to good news
jokes.explanation: Shows Amy still has some residual aggression from the nectar, but fairly basic

jokes.text: Well, looks like Fry's three-way is down to a one-way. If he's lucky.
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela no longer interested in Fry romantically
jokes.punchline: Bender's mathematical assessment of Fry's romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Good Bender observation with clever mathematical framework for sexual prospects

jokes.text: What are you doing at your place of work?
jokes.setup: Amy and Leela showing up for their championship match
jokes.punchline: Treating professional sports venue as unusual place for athletes to be
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about athletes actually showing up to work

jokes.text: Since this is a championship match, we are gonna need some lava!
jokes.setup: Announcing championship match special features
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of deadly molten rock as sports equipment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the already dangerous sport with completely over-the-top addition

jokes.text: I once killed a sea turtle with these boots.
jokes.setup: Murderfly explaining why lava doesn't hurt her
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of killing endangered species with footwear
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing casual violence as backstory explanation for toughness

jokes.text: He's even more beautiful than before!
jokes.setup: Fry transforming into giant butterfly
jokes.punchline: Amy finding his monstrous form attractive
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Amy's continued attraction despite horrific transformation

jokes.text: Oh, cripes! They're mating!
jokes.setup: Fry as butterfly approaches Amy and Leela
jokes.punchline: Kif's horrified realization of what's happening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif reaction to witnessing disturbing romantic situation

jokes.text: I am so sorry for allowing this lurid sex act to taint an otherwise delightful day of family-friendly violence.
jokes.setup: Announcer apologizing for inappropriate content
jokes.punchline: Treating violence as family-friendly while sex is inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of media standards - violence is acceptable but sexuality isn't. Excellent social commentary

jokes.text: That is some freaky bug-on-bug action. I feel a little left out.
jokes.setup: Everyone watching the mating scene in horror
jokes.punchline: Bender wishes he could participate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - manages to make disturbing situation about his own desires

jokes.text: He has absolutely no brain activity. Look! He lost the strange insect parts. He's back to normal!
jokes.setup: Professor examining transformed Fry
jokes.punchline: Implying Fry's normal state is having no brain activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Fry's stupidity as his defining characteristic. Perfect Professor delivery and logic

tmdb_overview: The Professor's fast-fashion clothes are a smash hit, but an environmental disaster.
